title: Prosaptia hornei
taxonomy: Prosaptia hornei is classified within the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Prosaptia, which is a group of ferns. Specifically, Prosaptia hornei is a species of epiphytic fern found in tropical regions. The classification of Prosaptia hornei is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Phylum: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, and Family: Polypodiaceae. This classification helps to identify and understand the unique characteristics of this specific fern species.

intro: Prosaptia hornei, also known as the Australian maidenhair fern, is a species of fern in the family Lindsaeaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis and protect against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Prosaptia hornei include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, Prosaptia hornei is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications such as creams and salves, due to its purported ability to soothe and calm irritated skin.
